notes: A materialized thread/read(includeTurns=true) projection was captured after one approved turn/start request.
The returned thread contains one turn with the user message and no agent output shape.
turn/completed reported the turn as failed with a 401 backend auth error, but thread/read projected that same turn as status=completed with error=null and userMessage-only items.
This proves materialized thread projection exists, but it does not prove the current advisory fallback can recover agent output from auth-failed or error turns.
No server-initiated requests were observed in the no-auth lifecycle slice.
No server-initiated requests were observed in the approved one-turn model-mediated slice before the backend auth failure.
Because a model-mediated turn did run, server-request runtime evidence is no longer blocked on approval alone, but the envelope taxonomy remains unproven.

newly_satisfied_items: Materialized thread/read(includeTurns=true) projection exists for a thread with one turn under scratch CODEX_HOME.
The exact approved turn/start request shape is captured as live runtime evidence.
The no-auth and model-mediated slices both recorded that no server-request envelopes were emitted in these paths.
still_missing_items: Evidence that a materialized thread projection contains recoverable agent output shape for fallback use.
Live server-request envelopes from a successful or approval-blocked model/tool turn.
Compatibility judgment for actual server-request envelopes against the local parser/runtime boundary.
